rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in component type scopes for assumptions and guarantees that reference visible ports, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the AADL model when writing AGREE contracts.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL scopes: type annexes for component-level contracts, implementation annexes for subcomponent-level bindings.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or data types not present in the analyzed AADL model.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible features and ports at their respective scope levels.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use `const`, `eq`, `ass`, and `gua` for declarations and assertions.
Logical operators in AGREE include `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes unless they are valid outputs or assignments.
Do not reference non-visible identifiers or data types that are not part of the AADL model in AGREE contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Model analyst extraction of AADL structure
